failure_modes.name: Undefined workflow structure
failure_modes.description: The service sequence, decision rules, and escalation logic are too implicit to hold under live pressure.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: workflow design failure expressed through restaurant service flow

failure_modes.name: Host flow breakdown
failure_modes.description: Quoting, pacing, seating release, and reservation handling are not tight enough to shape arrival demand cleanly.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: expectation-setting and handoff logic in hospitality intake

failure_modes.name: Deployment misalignment
failure_modes.description: Coverage, sectioning, and peak-role placement do not match the real demand pattern or skill mix of the shift.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: demand-capacity mismatch expressed in FOH deployment

failure_modes.name: Pass communication gap
failure_modes.description: Timing language, pickup ownership, and cross-station handoff rules at the pass are too weak to move food cleanly.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: handoff failure expressed in kitchen-to-floor transfer

failure_modes.name: Prep system breakdown
failure_modes.description: Prep logic, depletion visibility, and replenishment discipline are not strong enough to keep service execution stable.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: readiness failure expressed through prep and replenishment

failure_modes.name: Shift readiness gap
failure_modes.description: The venue enters service without a reliable readiness check across stations, people, stock, and live priorities.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: readiness failure expressed through service start conditions

failure_modes.name: Leadership briefing gap
failure_modes.description: Critical pre-service direction is missing, so the team starts without a shared read on pressure points, priorities, or constraints.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: leadership rhythm failure expressed through pre-shift briefing absence

failure_modes.name: Manager presence gap
failure_modes.description: Managers are not visibly positioned where live reset, coaching, and escalation are needed during high-risk moments.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: leadership rhythm failure expressed through floor presence

failure_modes.name: Open-close control gap
failure_modes.description: Opening and closing are treated as chores instead of enforced control loops that protect the next service cycle.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: readiness and control-loop failures expressed through reset discipline

failure_modes.name: Control loop gap
failure_modes.description: Void, comp, waste, stock, and other operational leakage are visible but not reviewed tightly enough to drive correction.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: control-loop failure expressed through restaurant operating controls

failure_modes.name: Inventory accuracy gap
failure_modes.description: Counts, depletion updates, and 86 status are not trustworthy enough for service teams to act with confidence.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: commercial discipline and control-loop weakness in stock truth

failure_modes.name: Menu complexity overload
failure_modes.description: The menu or service promise asks the operation to carry more complexity than the current system can execute reliably.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: expectation and complexity failure expressed through the offer

failure_modes.name: Bar workflow breakdown
failure_modes.description: Bar setup, sequencing, and priority rules are not robust enough to support live service demand and floor handoffs.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: workflow and handoff failure expressed in beverage execution

failure_modes.name: Insufficient training protocol
failure_modes.description: Standards, role expectations, and recovery behaviors are not reinforced enough to survive into live service execution.
failure_modes.source_ref: core-v2: capability gap expressed through hospitality team training

response_patterns.name: Service flow reset
response_patterns.description: Make the core service sequence explicit, visible, and executable under real pressure.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: redesign workflow expressed through service flow

response_patterns.name: Host pacing reset
response_patterns.description: Constrain quoting, arrival pacing, and seating release so front-door promises stay inside actual capacity.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: constrain promises and strengthen demand shaping at the door

response_patterns.name: Deployment reset
response_patterns.description: Rebuild sectioning, role placement, and peak-window coverage around the real shape of service demand.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: align roles and coverage in the live venue

response_patterns.name: Pass communication reset
response_patterns.description: Install explicit timing language, handoff rules, and pickup discipline across the pass.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: strengthen handoffs at the kitchen-floor boundary

response_patterns.name: Prep and control reset
response_patterns.description: Restore prep visibility, depletion awareness, and live control points before service instability compounds.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: restore readiness and tighten control loops around prep

response_patterns.name: Leadership rhythm reset
response_patterns.description: Create repeatable pre-shift, in-shift, and post-shift leadership cadence so the floor gets real direction and closure.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: install operating rhythm for hospitality leadership

response_patterns.name: Manager presence reset
response_patterns.description: Put visible management attention where service risk is actually building, not where it is most comfortable to stand.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: align leadership coverage with real operating pressure

response_patterns.name: Open-close reset
response_patterns.description: Turn opening and closing into non-negotiable readiness and control rituals that protect the next cycle of work.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: restore readiness through enforced operating rhythm

response_patterns.name: Inventory accuracy reset
response_patterns.description: Make counts, 86 logic, and depletion updates trustworthy enough that service decisions stop relying on guesswork.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: tighten control loops and restore commercial discipline

response_patterns.name: Bar execution reset
response_patterns.description: Re-sequence bar setup, priority rules, and service handoff so beverage flow holds under peak pressure.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: redesign workflow at the beverage node

response_patterns.name: Training reset
response_patterns.description: Move standards, role clarity, and recovery behavior out of memory and into reinforced execution practice.
response_patterns.source_ref: core-v2: build capability and standardize execution

signal_failure_map.source_ref: core-v2: cycle delay is most often workflow fragility expressed live
core-v2: cycle delay often compounds when coverage is wrong for the peak
core-v2: live confusion usually means the sequence or decision rules are too implicit
core-v2: confusion also reveals standards that have not been reinforced enough
core-v2: absent briefing is the direct operational signal for a leadership rhythm gap at service start
core-v2: the same venue often lacks both pre-shift direction and live managerial coverage
core-v2: bottlenecks often reflect sequence design failure rather than isolated speed weakness
core-v2: bottlenecks also surface when prep readiness is not protecting line flow
core-v2: repeated complaints often persist where live recovery and floor guidance are too weak
core-v2: rising voids and comps are a direct leakage signal for weak control loops
core-v2: waste spikes should first be treated as control-loop weakness, not normal noise
core-v2: waste also rises when prep and replenishment logic are unstable

failure_pattern_map.source_ref: core-v2: workflow design failure should first activate service flow redesign
core-v2: some flow failures also require a coverage realignment to hold
core-v2: host flow failure should directly activate promise and pacing correction
core-v2: load mismatch at service level is first corrected through deployment realignment
core-v2: handoff failure at the pass should activate direct handoff reinforcement
core-v2: prep breakdown requires readiness restoration plus control points
core-v2: readiness gaps need a stronger service-start reset ritual
core-v2: readiness is also protected by better prep visibility and validation
core-v2: missing briefing is a direct leadership rhythm correction target
core-v2: visible management attention should be rebuilt where live risk accumulates
core-v2: open-close weakness should trigger an operating-rhythm reset around resets
core-v2: some restaurant control leakage centers on stock truth and depletion reliability
core-v2: control weakness often shares the same live visibility gaps as prep breakdowns
core-v2: inventory accuracy failure should directly activate stock-truth restoration
core-v2: excessive menu complexity often first shows up as service flow instability
core-v2: beverage-node workflow weakness needs a direct sequencing reset
core-v2: capability gaps are first corrected by reinforced training and explicit standards
